rows.title: Asset dependencies across code locations

rows.title: How to clenup table event_logs?

rows.title: Using PythonScriptComponent (or any other dagster feature), can I call a python script with different python dependencies than the dagster user-deployment?

rows.title: Is there a Celery-specific RunLauncher (without k8s)?
rows.reason: A correctly states there's no Celery-specific RunLauncher (Celery is an executor), while B hallucinates a nonexistent CeleryRunLauncher and misdescribes the architecture.

rows.title: Multiple code locations in Dagster OSS with shared code (resources, utils)

rows.reason: A defers on the table question; B conveys the right concept (one table for all ticks) but invents a nonexistent 'sensor_ticks' table/API and omits retention—both miss that job_ticks holds all ticks subject to retention.

rows.title: How to use `run_requests` and `dynamic_partitions_requests` with `SensorResult`

rows.reason: B addresses the questions and gets the default executor/flow right but is wrong that the agent isn't the launcher and that Celery works; A is honest but answers almost nothing.

rows.title: How to properly trigger a job once other are done?

rows.title: airbyte+ dbt + dagster: dbt upstream dependency issue
rows.reason: A correctly identifies the schedule only includes dbt assets and fixes it like the reference; B misdiagnoses by claiming lineage is undefined when the UI already shows it.

rows.title: Create dynamic assets on runtime
rows.reason: B correctly identifies and demonstrates dynamic partitions as the solution matching the reference, while A identifies the concept but fails to provide an actual implementation.

rows.title: Confused about code locations and kubernetes
rows.reason: Neither identifies DefaultRunLauncher for Q1; B is more on-target for OSS/helm and Q2 image fix despite a hallucinated config, while A only addresses Dagster+ container_context.
